name: Vienna Christmas Stollen
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Milk; scald and cool to lukewarm
recipeInstructions:
1. Scald the milk and let it cool to about 100°F.
2. Dissolve yeast in lukewarm water and add 1/4 cup cooled milk and 3 cups sifted flour. Cover with a cloth and let it rise until bubbles appear on the surface and it is ready to drop in the center.
3. Pour remaining milk over the sponge. Add egg yolks, sugar, and salt; beat well. Add 1 cup flour and beat again. Gradually add more flour (10-11 cups total) until a smooth dough forms. Some flours absorb more liquid than others.
4. Knead in sultanas, almonds, citron, lemon rind, and rum. Knead the dough until fruits and nuts are well distributed and smooth. Dust with flour and let rise in a warm place for 45 minutes.
5. Punch down the dough and let rest for 20 minutes. Divide into two pieces and knead each piece until smooth. Let rest for another 10 minutes.
6. Roll one piece of dough on a floured board into an elliptical shape, 6 inches long and 3 1/2 inches wide. The center should be slightly thicker (1/8 inch) and about 4 inches long. Fold the edges toward each other to form "lips," pressing down near the center.
7. Place the shaped dough on a buttered baking sheet, brush with melted butter, and fold one lip over the other. Press fingertips near the center and pull away from the ends, forming a waning moon shape. Repeat with the second piece of dough.
8. Let the Stollen rise until doubled in bulk (about 1 1/2 hours). Bake in a moderately hot oven (375°F) for 35-40 minutes. Do not overbake. Cool on racks and brush with butter before serving.
